course_description:
For students with little or no previous experience of Latin. The course teaches Latin grammar and basic syntax, and gives an introduction to the reading of Latin. NOTE: The course is sectioned to ensure a maximum class size of no more than 30.

LATN1110 is a two-unit (#2) introductory course, introducing the basic grammar and syntax of Classical Latin, with an emphasis on reading. It is designed for students with little or no previous experience of Latin. There are 4 hours of classes per week. As with learning any language, regular attendance and study are important.

PLEASE DO NOT MISS THE FIRST TWO WEEKS OF CLASSES. THEY ARE ESSENTIAL.

Class times are as follows:

Wednesday 1pm-2:50pm pm in 50 N202 and Friday 12pm-1:50pm 14-132

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:

course_contacts.notes: Please contact Dr McWilliam via email or microsoft teams for an in-person or zoom meeting if required

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
If you have any questions about the timetable, course or resources, please email Dr McWilliam: j.mcwilliam@uq.edu.au
Courses are taught in Blended Mode rather than in lecture/tutorial format, so in each 2 hour block, students will do different combinations of grammar and interactive exercises, reading and parsing to reinforce weekly and cumulative grammar and vocabulary.
IF YOU ARE UNDER 18, you will not be able to log in to the electronic resources on campus unless you obtain permission- please contact ITS (Information Technology Services) as soon as possible, preferably before classes in Week 1.

course_aims: By the end of LATN1110 you should:

learning_outcomes.description: read and analyse the reading passages in the LATN1110 Reader

demonstrate via exercises, quizzes, reading and parsing an understanding of the morphology, syntax and vocabulary of Latin up to Section 2C contained in the Grammar Reader put together for LATN1110, and the Latin reading passages in Reading Latin Text up to 2C.

assessment_details.title: In Person Mid-Semester Exam
assessment_details.due_date: 15/04/2026 1:00 pm The mid-semester exam will be held in class in person.
assessment_details.task_description: MID-SEMESTER EXAM Content and Requirements: translation from Latin into English of passages from the Introduction to Section 1E of the LATN1110 Text Reader. English translations must demonstrate your understanding of the Latin parsing to show your understanding of the vocabulary, grammar, and syntax contained in the texts translated weekly in class, so it is important that you do your weekly translations and c ome to class to discuss your translations and ask questions translation into English of sentences/passages based on the learning vocabulary, grammar, and syntax of the Introduction to Section 1E . English translations must demonstrate your understanding of the Latin grammatical questions/exercises of the type covered in class and for assignments based on the Introduction- 1E , designed to test your increasing knowledge of the vocabulary, grammar, and syntax covered in these sections. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Skill Building: Class Participation and Practice
assessment_details.task_description: Students are required to translate set texts assigned before class, as listed by Section in the Course Profile and on Blackboard. These texts will be re translated and discussed in class in conjunction with points of grammar, syntax, literary style, and where relevant, historical and/or cultural context, and will also be re-examined in the mid-semester and final exam. Each student will also be assigned a specific set of lines to translate individually in class, which will be assigned in week 1 and throughout the semester. Points of grammar, syntax, and vocabulary acquisition will also be reinforced though class exercises which are designed to provide immediate feedback, focus on points of grammar and syntax, literary technique, and relevant historical and/or cultural contexts. Students are encouraged to use the class time to ask questions encountered during preparation or that arise in class during translation, discussion, or during the completion of class exercises. Although AI and Machine translation could potentially be used for this task, at beginner level it does not help students to master the skills needed, and in some cases may be misleading and time consuming. Students are advised to use the specific Learning Materials have been developed for this course, which will be explained in Week 1 of the course. Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Students are required to be in class to translate their set lines: written work cannot be submitted in lieu of in-person attendance. Please print out your translated lines and hand them in at the beginning of class: while these will not be assigned an individual mark as this exercise is about providing feedback through discussion, and they form part of your overall participation mark. In person class participation is demonstrated through active participation in all components of the class: demonstration of prepared translations of set texts for weekly classes on Wednesday and Friday, in-class translation and discussion of set lines of the prepared texts, active engagement in discussions directed by the lecturer, and participation in other class learning activities.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.task_description: Part 1 20%: Students are required to complete six (6) take home assignments and submit them on Blackboard by the dates and times specified. The due dates are available under the assessment tab on the LATN1110 Blackboard site. Please note that ALL 6 assignments must be submitted to achieve a passing grade for this course, and a geninue attempt must be made (i.e., submission of an assignment with no answers is not considered a genuine submission) Assignments comprise: translation from Latin into English of sentences and passages based on the learning vocabulary and grammar studied in the week(s) preceding each assignment. Translations must demonstrate your understanding of the Latin parsing of words to show your understanding of the text (vocabulary, grammar, and syntax) translation into Latin of some simple sentences and/or a short unseen passage based on the learning vocabulary. Translations must demonstrate your understanding of the Latin. grammatical exercises of the type covered in class. Assignments are designed to test your increasing knowledge of the vocabulary, grammar, and syntax covered during the course. It is important to read the feedback you receive each week, reflect on what you need to work on, and ask questions if you are still unsure. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. This includes the use of programs such as Google Translate, which are usually inaccurate. The assignments are designed to assist you with building the necessary skills for the mid-semester and final exams. Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course Part 2: Select Option A or B: These assessment items are designed to help you to acquire vocabulary and to master declension and conjugation patterns in particular Option A QUIZZES 15% EITHER 1083 or more uqlatin quizzes (located on UQ Extend) completed throughout semester or 2 timed Blackboard quizzes held in conjunction with the mid-semester and final exam uqlatin quizzes: Students must complete 3 genuine attempts at all the following types of quizzes on uqlatin, with their overall number of quizzes attempted equaling 1083 (there are 361 quizzes in total): noun declension adjective/pronoun declension verb conjugation word matching/vocab translation translation/identification of prepositions and idiomatic constructions If you elect to take this option, you must start your quiz practice in Week 1 or you will not reach the required number of quizzes by the end of semester. STUDENTS WHO DO NOT TAKE UP THIS OPTION MUST COMPLETE OPTION B Two (2) timed Blackboard Quizzes held in conjunction with the mid-semester and final exams Each quiz is 10 mins long, and only one attempt is permitted for each quiz. The mark for each quiz will be added together and divided by 2 to calculate the final mark out of 15. noun declension adjective/pronoun declension verb conjugation word matching/vocab translation translation/identification of prepositions and idiomatic constructions Details of both assessment tasks are available on Blackboard These assessment tasks evaluate students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course EITHER 1083 or more uqlatin quizzes (located on UQ Extend) completed throughout semester or 2 timed Blackboard quizzes held in conjunction with the mid-semester and final exam uqlatin quizzes: Students must complete 3 genuine attempts at all the following types of quizzes on uqlatin, with their overall number of quizzes attempted equaling 1083 (there are 361 quizzes in total): noun declension adjective/pronoun declension verb conjugation word matching/vocab translation translation/identification of prepositions and idiomatic constructions If you elect to take this option, you must start your quiz practice in Week 1 or you will not reach the required number of quizzes by the end of semester. These assessment tasks evaluate students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Please submit on Blackboard following the instructions provided
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Please apply online following HPI guidelines.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
Assignments submitted late will be marked. Please note that a genuine attempt at answering all questions must be made for all assignments. An assignment which is blank will not be counted as a genuine attempt.
Please note that it is not normally possible to extend or defer the uqlatin quizzes unless you have been keeping up all semester, so please plan your time. If you have been granted a deferral for either of the written exams, please contact Dr McWilliam and she will provide access to the Blackboard Quizzes.

learning_activities.topic: (Week 1): Course Introduction, Methods of Studying Latin, Basic Grammatical Concepts Introduction; methods of studying Latin; basic grammatical concepts and terminology Learning outcomes: L01, L02

(Week 2): Section 1A Please prepare for both classes the text of Section 1A from the LATN1110 Text Reader. Requirements will be explained in Week 1 of the course. Don't worry if you can't get through the whole text- your skills will increase rapidly during the course. In class, we will learn about grammatical concepts by doing class exercises and re-translating the text together to reinforce these concepts and build vocabulary and reading skills. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

(Week 3): Section 1B Please prepare for both classes the text of Section 1B from the LATN1110 Text Reader. Again, don't worry if you can't get through the whole text- your skills will increase rapidly during the course. In class, we will learn about grammatical concepts found in this passage by doing class exercises and re-translating the text together both to reinforce these concepts and build further vocabulary and reading skills. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

(Week 4): Section 1C Please prepare for both classes the text of Section 1C from the LATN1110 Text Reader. In class, we will learn about grammatical concepts found in this passage by doing class exercises and re-translating the text together both to reinforce these concepts and build further vocabulary and reading skills. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

(Week 5): Section 1D Please prepare for both classes the text of Section 1D from the LATN1110 Text Reader. In class, we will learn about grammatical concepts found in this passage by doing class exercises and re-translating the text together both to reinforce these concepts and build further vocabulary and reading skills. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

(Week 6) Section 1E Please prepare for the text of Section 1E from the LATN1110 Text Reader. In class, we will learn about grammatical concepts found in this passage by doing class exercises and re-translating the text together both to reinforce these concepts and build further vocabulary and reading skills. Please note that there is no class on Good Friday, which is a public holiday. Please complete the Class Exercises online and continue preparation for the mid-semester exam Learning outcomes: L01, L02

(Week 7) Mid-Semester Exam and Section 1E Please prepare for Friday the text of Section 1F from the LATN1110 Text Reader. We will do some grammar on Wednesday after the mid-semester exam, but no text reading. In class, we will learn about grammatical concepts found in this passage by doing class exercises and re-translating the text together both to reinforce these concepts and build further vocabulary and reading skills. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

(Week 8) Section 1G Please translate the text of Section 1G from the LATN1110 Text Reader. In class, we will learn about grammatical concepts found in this passage by doing class exercises and re-translating the text together both to reinforce these concepts and build further vocabulary and reading skills. We will do some grammar on Wednesday after the mid-semester exam Learning outcomes: L01, L02

(Week 9) Section 2A Part 1 Please prepare the text of Section 2A to approximately line 75 from the LATN1110 Text Reader for both classes. In class, we will learn about grammatical concepts found in this passage by doing class exercises and re-translating the text together both to reinforce these concepts and build further vocabulary and reading skills. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

(Week 10) Section 2A Part 2 Please prepare for the remainder of the text of Section 2A from the LATN1110 Text Reader. In class, we will learn about grammatical concepts found in this passage by doing class exercises and re-translating the text together both to reinforce these concepts and build further vocabulary and reading skills. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

(Week 11) Section 2B Please prepare the text of Section 2B from the LATN1110 Text Reader for both classes. In class, we will learn about grammatical concepts found in this passage by doing class exercises and re-translating the text together both to reinforce these concepts and build further vocabulary and reading skills. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

(Week 12) Section 2C Please prepare the text of Section 2C from the LATN1110 Text Reader for both classes. In class, we will learn about grammatical concepts found in this passage by doing class exercises and re-translating the text together both to reinforce these concepts and build further vocabulary and reading skills. Learning outcomes: L01, L02
(Week 13) Revision In the last week of Semester, we will translate any outstanding text, and do some more focused revision for the Final Exam. Learning outcomes: L01, L02
